sections.heading: Performance Overview
sections.content: NEXUS performance is heavily dependent on AI inference speed and camera capture latency. The system is designed to run at 30+ FPS, but requires specific optimizations based on the host hardware to maintain low latency and high throughput.

sections.heading: Model Benchmarks (RTX 3080 Reference)
rows: CPU fallback — No GPU available (Very slow)

sections.heading: Optimization Strategies
items: Use TensorRT Engines: Always export models to .engine format. PyTorch .pt models are 5-10x slower as they run as CPU fallbacks.
Lower Camera Resolution: Dropping RESOLUTION from [1280, 720] to [640, 480] in Settings provides a massive speed boost and reduces capture latency on lower-end hardware.
Mitigate CPU & USB Bottlenecks: Camera capture runs on the CPU. Connect cameras to separate physical USB 3.0 ports to lower capture latency. Sharing a USB hub increases delay.
Free Up GPU Memory: Close other GPU-intensive applications, including games, other ML models, and browsers with hardware acceleration.

sections.heading: Latency Bottleneck Detection via Logs
sections.code:
# Enable DEBUG level logging in main.py to view per-frame timing
import logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)

# Analyze the ──── Frame Timing ──── output in the logs:
# [DEBUG] capture_time = 8.2ms   -> If > 15ms: Check USB 3.0 ports, lower resolution
# [DEBUG] inference_time = 18.4ms -> If dominating: Switch to lighter model or rebuild .engine
# [DEBUG] dispatch_time = 0.3ms  -> If > 5ms: Check for serial port lag or baud mismatch
